title: Spot and fix atom-construction in telemetry tags
prompt:
The following Telemetry wrapper creates atoms dynamically from user-provided tag names — each new request type creates a new atom:

```elixir
defmodule MyApp.Telemetry.Tag do
  def report(metric_name, value, tags \\ %{}) do
    event = [:my_app, :telemetry, String.to_atom(metric_name)]
    measurements = %{value: value}
    metadata = for {k, v} <- tags, into: %{}, do: {String.to_atom(k), v}
    :telemetry.execute(event, measurements, metadata)
  end
end
```

Rewrite it so (a) `metric_name` is mapped to a fixed allowlist of known metric atoms at compile time, (b) `tags` keys are validated against a fixed set, (c) unknown metrics or tags result in an error tuple rather than creating new atoms. Write the fix to `lib/my_app/telemetry/tag.ex`.
tier_rationale: Hard — atom exhaustion in a less-obvious telemetry context.
